key_insight: A large language model, combined with Lean 4, produced a machine-verified proof of the FGG conjecture for depth-p QAOA on the ring of disagrees, confirming the exact ratio (2p+1)/(2p+2).
engineering_relevance: The work shows that AI-assisted theorem proving can move from theorem discovery to end-to-end certified proofs for hard problems in quantum optimization and other computational science domains.
method: Formalized the QAOA setting and known results in Lean 4.
Reduced the conjecture to one open mathematical statement.
Used an agentic LLM+Lean feedback loop to construct and mechanically verify the missing proof.
evidence_signal: The evaluation is end-to-end proof verification in Lean 4, with the abstract reporting that the proof was checked mechanically rather than empirically benchmarked.
watch_item: The structural statement still requires human validation to ensure the formal Lean encoding matches the intended mathematical conjecture.
paper_title: A Machine-Verified Proof of a Quantum-Optimization Conjecture
